title: dactylorhiza umbrosa var. ochroleuca
intro: Dactylorhiza umbrosa var. ochroleuca, also known as the yellow-spotted marsh orchid, is a rare and endangered subspecies of orchid native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to treat various health issues such as respiratory problems and skin conditions. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza umbrosa var. ochroleuca may include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and boosting the immune system. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tincture or decoction, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal treatment.
